revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:布帝耳神]]


{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| children = {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qid=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref><ref name="herbert-2010a" />{{rp|277-278}}
| parents ={{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}<ref name="herbert-2010a" />{{rp|277-278}}; {{ill|Funozuno|qid=Q65266238|1=Funozuno}}| spouse = {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}<ref name="herbert-2010a" />{{rp|277-278}}
| gender = {{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}
| consort = {{ill|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|1=Omizunu}}
| offspring = {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}
}}
'''Futemimi''' was a Japanese deity<ref name="-a-history-of-japan-日本歴史-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Futemimi • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/futemimi/ |access-date=2023-10-21 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="herbert-2010a">{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-dd-21}}</ref>{{rp|277-278}}

She is a goddess of clothing<ref name="-a-history-of-japan-日本歴史-2023a" />.

She is the daugther of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=}} and the wife of {{ill|Omizunu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|qq=}} and mother of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=}}<ref name="herbert-2010a" />{{rp|277-278|qq=draft}}

==Overview==

{{Infobox deity
| Name = Futemimi no Kami
| Classification = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}
| Full Name = 布帝耳神 (Futemimi no Kami)
| Other Names = 布弖弥美
| Deity Of = Clothing
| Parent = {{ill|Funozunono no Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozunono no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}
| Spouse = {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}
| Children = {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qid=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}
| Shrine = {{ill|Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|ja|長浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|qq=}}
| Mentioned In = {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}
}}
'''Futemimi no Kami''' (布帝耳神, Futemimi) is a {{ill|Goddess|en|Goddess|qid=Q205985|lt=goddess|1=Goddess}} who appears in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Futemimi no Kami is described as the wife of {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}<ref name="herbert-2010a3" />{{rp|277-278}}. According to the records of {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|qid=Q11604651|lt=Awaga Shrine|1=Awaga Shrine}} in the ''{{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}}'', she is referred to as ''Futemimi'' (布弖弥美). 

She is the mother of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qid=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}<ref name=":0">Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref name=":1">Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref><ref name="herbert-2010a2" />{{rp|277-278}}, the deity of clothing, and is closely associated with textile and fabric-related divinities. It is theorized that her parent deity, Funozunono no Kami, represents the material fibers used for clothing, further cementing her association with garments. 

The etymology of "Fute" remains unclear, but some interpretations suggest a derivation from the "Funo" in ''Funozunono''. "Mimi" could signify "spirit" or "divinity," derived from the term "霊" (spirit). Another theory posits that her name originated as ''Nunoobi-mimi'' (布帯御霊, Spirit of the Cloth Belt), with scribal errors in Chinese characters leading to the version recorded in the ''Kojiki''.<ref>''Kokugakuin University - Center for Kojiki Studies,'' Entry: Futemimi no Kami</ref>

== Genealogy ==

Futemimi no Kami is the daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|1=Funozuno|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|13=User:Immanuelle/Funozuno|qq=}}<ref name="herbert-2010a4" />{{rp|277-278}}. She is married to {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}<ref name="herbert-2010a3" />{{rp|277-278}} and bears their child,  {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qid=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /><ref name="herbert-2010a2" />{{rp|277-278}},.

== Shrines ==
Futemimi no Kami is enshrined at:
* {{ill|Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|ja|長浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahama Shrine (Izumo)|qq=}} (Nagahama, Nishizono-cho, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) – enshrined alongside other deities.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Jūnanasetsu no Kami|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūnanasetsu no Kami|1=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65249569}}
* {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}

== External Links ==

* [http://www.nagahamajinja.com/ Nagahama Shrine (Official Website)]
* [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/88140005e43021e12793883ec3358da50ea9e65a.html Nagahama Shrine – Shimane Prefecture Shrine Association]
